transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Good morning, good morning.
Good morning.
Good morning.
I think this is going to be an empty one today.
Andy's on the flight.
don't know if Ada's back, but I know Joe's out.
So, we can probably just do this a little different, I guess.
How's everyone just doing overall?
I know it was a crazy week.
A lot of things were moving around.
A lot of people had to pick up things that they weren't already doing, and then they basically had just started.
So, how's everyone doing overall workload-wise?
Is there anything they need help with today to get it out by the end of the week?
Like, just, where's everyone at?
I can start.
I was a little bit pulled behind on the articles for Augment Code, but today I finished with the five pending ones, and I'm moving forward with SentinelOne as well.
Awesome.
For those, Marcel, I'll send you, sorry, I got swamped yesterday, but we're basically just going to focus on the list of goals and comparisons.
you.
Thank Thank
And I'm going to go and scrunch today and pull like the top 10, 15 sources from competitors in whatever format those articles were.
We're just going to create something with a similar topic and structure and style because those are the ones that are working and getting the most clicks.
And all they care about right now is seeing signals from that.
So I'll work on that this morning and send it over to you.
And then, again, we're going to try to ramp up the volume next week to 30.
So I kind of want to get ahead of it.
And we're working on getting you some help on the article production as well.
Perfect.
Trying to find someone there.
So that should be temporary.
How much are doing for 10 on the 1, Marisol?
This week, I was working with the assignments, cleaning up the content OS and mocking.
good Good morning,
I have the design of the CVE pages, and today I want to polish that to make sure that the content fits the structure, the proposed structure.
Okay, cool.
That's good.
Let us know if you need any more help.
Aida, I saw that you just joined.
What's up?
We missed you a ton.
Um, because Joe's out, we're kind of just catching up on what everyone did.
Like, everyone had to pick up extra things.
So, like, where everything's at, if there's anything pressing people need help with, I'm assuming you're just getting back into it, right?
Yeah, so technically I'm still out today, but I got back a little earlier today, and I thought I'd hop on just to see what's going on, um, like, in preparation for next week.
Um, so yeah, just, like, looking to catch up and see what's going on this week.
Cool.
Yeah, that makes sense.
This is going to be less account-by-account, because Joe's out was recovering, and so more, like...
Let's cover the pressing things to get it out by the end of the week.
And I'm going to dive into and send an update later today on anything I think needs to help and needs to be picked up on.
Sydney, how's everything with you?
Everything's going good.
just getting caught up on Ambient, considering it was a customer.
And then after that, do we have any specific action items for Okta, other than updating based on the Starbucks value thing that they sent over?
No, that one's one that I've to spend some time thinking through.
It's just we're meeting with the two SDRs once we get put in contact with them.
And then I'm meeting with engineering, or we can meet with engineering to figure out like an actual workflow around it.
But then it's just experimenting with the artifacts we built and how good.

We can get the prep brief for the SDR, including Tiff's comments, because I know she said some was long, was more of like an account brief, so then two, like an account brief and an SDR pre-call brief that's shorter, punchier, what they need to cover, more of a qualifying candidate, and then she liked the longer account one.

So just playing with those a little bit more, because we're going to show engineering the output of what we got that looks great, and then run them through everything we did to get there, and then they're going to build the workflow around that, because it's just brand new.
So it's hard to speak to it, and they won't understand until we show them, like, everything we've done in the cloud projects and how we did it.
Okay, okay, got it.
There's another question on Okta.
Sumptimal 1 is going smoothly, Marisol, thank you so much for keeping that up, though I see now that she is stalled.
But yeah, other than that, everything's going good.
And if I have questions, I'm not going to.
Amazing.
Yeah, I'm going to play around with it today, too, so I'll send you an update at the of the day on the progress.
Sorry, I remember the question.
Did you get access to the clay table?
Because I think I got it to my personal account.
I did get access, yes.
Okay, awesome.
That's another quick win, playing around with clay and figuring out how to help her, because she back-handled me on the side, asked if we had any in-house clay experts to help her.
But, again, I don't want to get too distracted.

already doing so many different custom things for her, so maybe just focusing on these artifacts and this first workflow, and I'll play around with clay and see how it can help her, but not make it anything formal.
Cool.
Ade, was your first week, pretty much, or maybe second, and it was one of the crazier ones, so how's everything going with you?
Yeah, I think crazy is something, right?
It's getting pretty fine, though.
There's been a lot of, like,

Context switching, but I've had a ton of help and guidance on stuff, and thankfully, our documentation culture is really good here, so most of the time, I can find the information I need on Notion, or I'll just bug Marisol or something.
So yeah, it's been going really well.
In terms of pending stuff, end of week, I think most are fine.
I'm supposed to do a bit of like tagging for Diligent today and send an update to the client's channel regarding like how many more refreshes we have marked, you know, for their approval and stuff.
But yeah, and I think Outreach, they sent something that was like high priority, like yesterday night, my time anyways, they want us to, I think, Ada, you might probably want to take a look at that.
But they, you know, I think two of their competitors had like a merger or something, and they want to like have some sort of reactive content geared towards that, blogs, I don't know if it's out of.
Scope, but he asked that we deprioritize some of the refreshes for next week and prioritize those blogs.
Yeah, and regarding this stuff that we sent out this week.
Sorry, super quick.
Clary and Sales Loft merge.
Sales Loft is always their biggest competitor, so that's a huge deal.
Yeah, so whatever we need to do, jump on that to help them.
Very timely.
Yeah, they say they want it for next week.
Yeah, so it would need a response.
But regarding the stuff we sent them, like the product release blog, David liked that one.
I think they left a few minor things, and I think Joe addressed those already.
Then for the refresh we did, they left a few other comments, so I'm going to fix those today.
But she said she loved it as well, so I think that's going fine.
So the priority is just to get them the blogs and respond.

So the merger next week for Tavern, okay, great, for Tavern, we are pretty much set, I have done, I think I have, I've done audience competitors, regular competitor research as well, everything is on Notion, thank you Marisol for the guidance, I've done the content OS as well, version one anyways, it's a very niche space, so there's like a lot of room to position them, right, I think the biggest competitor has like a pretty low domain, like ranking as well, so like there's like lots of room for Tavern to grow, and they have a lot of backlinks, considering how much content they have, so just lots of opportunities there, I highlighted all of those in the reports, so that's up for review, I think like I said, Atlas workspace for them is also set up, so yeah, it's just pretty much for, you know, I think Ada to review those, so if you have anything for me to work on them today, I can get that done,
What else?
Diligent, I think they liked the refreshes we did for them.
They left a few comments.
I think they wanted us to hold off on one blog because I think they might be doing a bit of like product repositioning on that.
So they want us to hold off on refreshing that before.
But for the other ones, I'm going to just, you know, work on like the minor edits today and send them, update them.
I did have one issue.
Joe asked me to set up like SEO testing for the refresh that I published today.
I don't have access to the, there's like a loom or a Fathom thingy.
I don't have access to it.
So I requested access, but I don't know who made that recording.
I don't know whether it was Dave that made that or something.
Yeah, I can help you with that.
Oh, okay.
That would be great.
I've requested access to the loom.
So if you could grant, it was not loom, it was a Fathom recording actually.
So if you could grant that, that would be great.
But I think that's pretty much everything.
Diligent, however, and out.
Those were the main things.
Besides that, I'm pretty calm.
Yeah, okay, AirBytes.
AirBytes is, I'm trying to remember if there was anything on AirBytes.
It's pretty much just fixing the issues with the refreshes, to be honest.
The last batch was done pending the internal link issues on like five of them.
But I tagged those for you, George.
I think you saw them already, the ones that have actually been published.
And I tagged the others from the previous batches because I saw that they hadn't been done yet.
So yeah, I think we should be fine on that end.
But yeah, that's pretty much it for me.
Got it.
And yeah, that AirBytes meeting is this morning in about an hour.
so it's presenting everything we've done for the refreshes to date, but also the strategy moving forward that we've always talked about all along.
So let it be good.
Thanks for closing out those refreshes.
was super helpful.
Cool.
Yeah, I mean, for me, I know we all kind of went around the horn trying to catch up on Ambien got pushed.
Ambien
The kickoff from Monday, dates changed a bit, and then we actually ended up having it yesterday, so just diving in a little deeper.
They're a really cool, unique company, and they seem like two smart people to work with, and they're both new to the business and to the company.
The head of marketing is two months in, and then I'm forgetting the role of the other person on the call, Mauricio, who reports to him, but I think it was like one or two weeks in.

And there's a ton of opportunity, they have massive logos, do security cameras that track physical objects and threats, and then report them in real time, and they have all the biggest logos you can think of.
And then they also do personal security for top tech CEOs that they couldn't even mention who they watch, or who they protect.
And so, let me see, I'm checking you, Ada.
Okay, you're asking.
So yeah, just working on Ambient there, Clerk, Clerk is going fine.
think Andy jumped in and took a stab at the V1 of the artifacts.
Ade, do you have a second of time today to look over Clerk's artifacts that were already generated?
Maybe later, Andy, I could take a look.
You could just send me, is it like on the client shared workspace?
Yeah, it's just in the shared workspace.
Okay, I could take a look later today.
Cool.
And then you had Tavern, we had a couple deep dives.
Clerk, we have some upcoming.
So things are moving.
I'm just trying to pick up the slack elsewhere as well.
And then focus on Augment Code, Surge, and Okta with Sydney.
So yeah, that's me.
know, again, Jo was out and she was managing a lot.
So everything that was top of mind for everyone here, I think we covered.
Besides Dave, I know you're doing some projects across clients here.
Is there anything you need help with from us or anything big that you're delivering this week?
Yeah, so I talked to Tyler today, this morning from Diligent.
It was actually pretty good.
So a key takeaway is from that meeting, reporting is a big concern for him.
So if we don't have a looker dashboard set up for that right away, then I'll get one set up ASAP.
I got Scrunch working correctly now on all of the accounts.
So I worked with that team yesterday to fix that.
So we can actually add Scrunch data.
So he's particularly...
How is it working incorrectly?
It was breaking.
So they had set it up to where it was only working for like two or three accounts.
So if you set up a new account, it wasn't part of the whole process.
So they've fixed it to where it works for all of our accounts, which is great.
You're saying when you try to pull it into...
The Looker?
Yeah, the API would reject the query because they had not granted permission to any of the new accounts, only the old accounts that we had.
So now the API will accept any new account that we have from GrowthX.
So that's super helpful because it just wasn't working before.
So we can now track prompts, visibility, sentiment, and stuff like that.
So I'll set that up on their Looker dashboard.
And then if you need to customize it, go ahead after it's like, you know, the baseline is set up for that.
Other things that he talked about were like, they have a lot of acquisitions and a lot of content migrations.
He has no visibility into what the old content inventory was for these old sites or how they were redirected.
So that's a major concern for him because he's like, okay, we're doing well on blog posts, but our product pages aren't doing well.
Like, you know, so I think there's...
Opportunity for us to upsell them on some sort of SEO, potentially, like technical SEO, or at least some technical overview of some things that could be an opportunity for us there.
Internal linking was also another concern for him.
They do a really bad job at it with their old blogs and their old content, so he needs help there.

And then, I guess, you know, I won't go verbatim all that, I've posted this in the channel, but the other one that stood out was like content strategy, coordinating his internal teams with content with GrowthX.
So like, he doesn't want, you know, us to create content or their teams, the internal teams to create content that competes with one another.

So I think as, and my feedback to him was like, hey, as this relationship matures, know, we'll know the content that you're publishing, and we'll have a better idea of internally what your content looks like and our content, so we can avoid situations like that.
But it could be worth just, you know, setting something up to where, you know.
We're at least have some visibility into the content that you're producing so we can provide input or something like that.
So, yeah, that's mainly it.
Nice.
Yeah, I mean, we're obviously all here who's involved, but like a way to be able to quickly look and track potential upsells, because that was super deep.
I don't know if I just went in and looked at the account in Notion, I would have known that.
So I'll add that to the notes every time, too, but that's good.
I know he also asked for a lot, so it's about balancing what he's asking for, what we want to deliver a little bit of to get them excited, and what we actually want to ask for an upsell on.
Cool.

Yeah, I don't know about you all, but I have a ton of outstanding items that would make my head feel better if I go clear them out, all related to the sprints, and then I can check in later today once I do.
There's a lot of moving parts, so that's kind of all.
All I've got, unless anyone else has anything they want to cover.
Looks like not.
Cool.
Cool.
Well, if not, we'll talk Monday.
Thanks, everybody.
Enjoy the weekend.
See you.
Bye, everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Strategy Sprint Standup to discuss ongoing projects, workloads, and pressing issues across multiple accounts.](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=54.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Team adapting to high workload and context switching due to key members being out](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [Significant progress on various accounts (Augment Code, SentinelOne, Okta, Ambient, Diligent, Tavern, AirBytes)](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=713.0)
  - [Outreach requesting urgent reactive content due to competitor merger (Clari and SalesLoft)](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
  - [Diligent expressing concerns about reporting, content migrations, and internal linking](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=946.0)

## Topics

### Team Workload and Adaptation

  - [Team managing increased responsibilities due to key members being out](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [New team members (e.g., Ademilade) adapting well despite challenging circumstances](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=419.0)
  - [Strong documentation culture helping team navigate tasks and find information](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=419.0)

### Account-Specific Updates

#### Augment Code & SentinelOne

  - [Marisol completed 5 pending articles for Augment Code](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Focus on top 10-15 sources from competitors for article creation](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Aiming to ramp up volume to 30 articles next week](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=145.0)

#### Okta

  - [Sydney working on updating based on Starbucks value proposition](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
  - [Experimenting with artifacts: account brief and shorter SDR pre-call brief](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Planning to meet with SDRs and engineering to establish workflow](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)

#### Ambient

  - [Kickoff meeting held yesterday after date changes](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Company specializes in security cameras tracking physical objects and threats](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=748.0)
  - [Opportunity identified due to massive logos and unique offerings](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)

#### Diligent

  - [Dave met with Tyler from Diligent, identifying reporting as a major concern](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
  - [Scrunch now working correctly for all accounts, enabling data addition](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
  - [Concerns raised about content migrations, internal linking, and content strategy coordination](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=946.0)

#### Tavern

  - [Ademilade completed audience and competitor research](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
  - [Content OS (version 1) prepared](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
  - [Identified opportunities for growth due to niche space and low competitor domain rankings](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)

#### AirBytes

  - [George preparing for meeting to present refresh strategy and progress](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=693.0)

#### Outreach

  - [Urgent request for reactive content due to Clari and SalesLoft merger](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
  - [Deprioritizing some refreshes to focus on merger-related blogs](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### Technical Updates

  - [Scrunch API fixed to work with all GrowthX accounts](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
  - [Looker dashboard setup needed for Diligent](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=863.0)

## Next Steps

  - [George to send update on Okta progress by end of day](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
  - [Ademilade to review Clerk's artifacts in the shared workspace](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=801.0)
  - [Dave to set up Looker dashboard for Diligent ASAP](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=863.0)
  - [Team to prioritize reactive content for Outreach regarding Clari-SalesLoft merger](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
  - [George to compile and track potential upsells based on client feedback](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1034.0)
  - [Team to balance delivering on client requests while identifying upsell opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/q2ArUufoFcBY5w5S_WqxryxBVvxhn-nZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1051.0)

action_items.description: Send Marcel top 10-15 competitor sources for Augment Code articles
Send update to Sydney on Okta progress by end of day
Tag Diligent content and send update to client channel re: refreshes for approval
Prioritize reactive content for Outreach re: Clari/SalesLoft merger
Grant Ademilade access to Fathom recording for SEO testing
Review Clerk artifacts in shared workspace later today
Set up Looker dashboard for Diligent with Scrunch data (prompts, visibility, sentiment)
